docs.title: Suddenly
docs.text:
Federated shared fiction network — where NPCs from one game can become player characters in another.

Players publish session reports, and mentioned characters can be claimed, adopted, or forked by other players across instances via ActivityPub.

docs.title: What is Suddenly?
docs.text:
Suddenly is a self-hostable federated platform for tabletop RPG players. It connects instances together so characters and stories can travel across communities.

Key concepts:

  • Reports — session write-ups published by players
  • Characters — PCs and NPCs that appear in reports
  • Games — campaigns and one-shots grouping reports
  • Federation — instances communicate via ActivityPub

docs.title: Character links
docs.text:
When a character from one game appears in another group's story, three relationship types are possible:

Type Description Result Claim "Your NPC was my PC all along" Retcon, NPC replaced Adopt "I'm taking over your NPC" NPC becomes a PC Fork "My PC is inspired by your NPC" New linked PC

docs.title: Self-hosting
docs.text:
Suddenly runs on any server with Python and PostgreSQL — no Docker required.

  • Deployment on a VPS (coming soon)
  • Deployment on Alwaysdata (coming soon)
  • Deployment with Docker (coming soon)

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
• Adding a translation
  • GitHub repository

docs.title: Design System
docs.text: Suddenly's visual language — tokens, typography, and UI components for both light and dark modes.

docs.title: Moodboard
docs.text:
Sombre, littéraire, dramatique. Typographie serif pour la narration, Inter pour l'interface. Les accents vifs (crimson, neon) émergent sur des fonds profonds comme des néons dans la nuit.

The two target aesthetics:

  • Dark — deep cosmos background (#0a0a0a), surfaces slightly lifted (#111111), crimson glow on cards, neon green for success states.
  • Light — warm parchment (#faf9f6), off-white cards, muted purple borders, crimson as primary accent.

docs.title: Semantic tokens (CSS variables)
docs.text: Token Variable Light Dark Background --c-bg #faf9f6 #0a0a0a Surface --c-surface #f0ede8 #111111 Card --c-card #ffffff #1a1a1a Card dark variant --c-card-dark #f5f2ec #222222 Border --c-border #d4cfe8 #2a2a2a Primary text --c-primary #1a1a2e #f0f0f0 Secondary text --c-secondary #4a4a6a #a0a0a0 Muted text --c-muted #8a8aaa #606060

docs.title: Fixed accent colors
docs.text: Name Hex Usage Crimson #e03558 Primary CTA, links, focus rings — both modes Crimson hover #c82a4a Button hover state Violet #7c3aed Per-page accent (profile pages, nav active) Neon #00e676 Success / available states in dark mode Success #16a34a Positive feedback Warning #d97706 Caution states Error #dc2626 Destructive actions, validation errors Info #6366f1 Informational states

docs.title: Typography
docs.text:
Three families — each with a distinct role:

Family Use Weights Inter UI — navigation, labels, buttons, body copy 400, 500, 600, 700 Crimson Text Narrative — H1/H2/H3 headings, report prose, blockquotes 400, 600, italic JetBrains Mono Code — inline and blocks 400, 500

Rule: Inter for all interface chrome. Crimson Text only where narrative voice matters — large headings and long-form text.

docs.title: Type scale — Light
docs.text:
Light mode

H1 — Crimson Text 600, 2.25rem

H2 — Crimson Text 600, 1.6rem

H3 — Crimson Text 600, 1.25rem

H4 — Inter 600, 1rem

H5 — Inter 600, 0.875rem

H6 — Inter 600 caps, 0.8rem

Body — Inter 400, 1rem / 1.6. The characters mentioned can be claimed, adopted, or forked by other players.

Small — Inter 400, 0.875rem. Secondary interface text, metadata, timestamps.

Caption — Inter 400, 0.75rem. Helper text, image captions.

Overline — Inter 600, 0.7rem, 3px tracking

Prose — Crimson Text 400, 1.1rem / 1.7. The night was long and the fire had gone cold when the stranger finally appeared at the threshold.

Prose italic — Crimson Text italic. "We do not choose our stories," she said, "they choose us."

Mono — JetBrains Mono, 0.875rem. const actor = await federation.resolve(url);

docs.title: Type scale — Dark
docs.text:
Dark mode

H1 — Crimson Text 600, 2.25rem

H2 — Crimson Text 600, 1.6rem

H3 — Crimson Text 600, 1.25rem

H4 — Inter 600, 1rem

H5 — Inter 600, 0.875rem

H6 — Inter 600 caps, 0.8rem

Body — Inter 400, 1rem / 1.6. The characters mentioned can be claimed, adopted, or forked by other players.

Small — Inter 400, 0.875rem. Secondary interface text, metadata, timestamps.

Caption — Inter 400, 0.75rem. Helper text, image captions.

Overline — Inter 600, 0.7rem, 3px tracking

Prose — Crimson Text 400, 1.1rem / 1.7. The night was long and the fire had gone cold when the stranger finally appeared at the threshold.

Prose italic — Crimson Text italic. "We do not choose our stories," she said, "they choose us."

Mono — JetBrains Mono, 0.875rem. const actor = await federation.resolve(url);

docs.title: Buttons
docs.text: Class Usage btn-primary Primary CTA — one per screen btn-secondary Secondary action alongside primary btn-ghost Tertiary / icon-only actions btn-danger Destructive actions (delete, reject) btn-sm Modifier — compact contexts btn-lg Modifier — hero sections Light mode Primary Secondary Ghost Danger Small Default Large Dark mode Primary Secondary Ghost Danger Small Default Large

docs.title: Badges
docs.text:
Character status badges — semantic colors consistent across both modes.

Class Meaning badge-available Character has no pending links badge-claimed Claim request pending or accepted badge-adopted Adoption accepted badge-forked Fork relationship established badge-pc Player Character (owned by a user) badge-pending Request awaiting response badge-rejected Request declined Light mode Available Claimed Adopted Forked PC Pending Rejected Dark mode Available Claimed Adopted Forked PC Pending Rejected

docs.title: Images & Avatars
docs.text:
Avatars follow the avatar-* size scale. Always provide a avatar-placeholder fallback when no image is available.

Class Size Usage avatar-sm 32 × 32 px Stacked groups, inline meta avatar-md 40 × 40 px List items, compact cards avatar-lg 48 × 48 px Card headers avatar-xl 64 × 64 px Profile page header avatar-placeholder any size Fallback — icon on bg-surface

docs.title: Avatar sizes
docs.text:
Same image at each scale. The border-radius: 9999px and object-fit: cover are applied by the avatar base class.

Light mode — with image sm · 32px md · 40px lg · 48px xl · 64px ✦ placeholder Dark mode — with image sm · 32px md · 40px lg · 48px xl · 64px ✦ placeholder

docs.title: Stacked avatar group
docs.text:
Used in game cards to show recent characters (-space-x-2, ring-2 ring-card). Overflow beyond 5 shown as +N chip.

Light mode +3 Dark mode +3

docs.title: Empty state
docs.text:
Used whenever a list has no items. Always includes icon + title, optionally description + CTA.

Light mode — with CTA

No games yet

Create your first game to start publishing session reports.

New game Dark mode — search empty

No characters found

Try different search terms or filters.

docs.title: Forms
docs.text:
Forms are wrapped in card card-body space-y-4. Each field follows the same structure: label → input → help/error.

Class Element Usage form-label <label> Field label — Inter 500, text-secondary form-input <input>, <textarea>, <select> Base input style form-input-error same elements Error state — crimson border + ring form-help <p> Helper text below field form-error <p> Validation error below field form-dropzone <div> File upload zone form-dropzone-link <label> Clickable part of dropzone

Optional fields carry (optional) in muted text next to the label.

docs.title: Settings navigation
docs.text:
Multi-page settings use a sidebar on desktop and horizontal scrollable tabs on mobile. The active item receives a bg-surface background and text-primary font-medium.

Anatomy — desktop sidebar + mobile tabs

Profile Federation Data Log out

Profile

Active settings page content.

Profile Federation Data Log out

Profile

Active settings page content.

Mobile — horizontal scrollable tabs

Profile Federation Data Log out Content area

Template — extends users/settings_base.html, override {% block settings_content %}:

{% extends "users/settings_base.html" %}
{% block title %}My page — {{ SITE_NAME }}{% endblock %}
{% block settings_content %}
  <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold text-primary mb-6">My page</h1>
  ...
{% endblock %}

Active state detection uses request.resolver_match.url_name.

docs.title: Icons
docs.text:
Suddenly uses three icon sources with distinct roles:

Library Prefix Role Lucide i-lucide-* UI chrome — navigation, actions, status Game Icons i-game-icons-* Thematic — empty states, section art, RPG concepts Simple Icons i-simple-icons-* Brand logos (Mastodon, ActivityPub)

All three are loaded via UnoCSS + Iconify. Icons are rendered as CSS masks — size via text-*, color via text-* tokens.

<!-- Lucide — UI -->
<span class="i-lucide-book-open text-xl text-secondary"></span>

<!-- Game Icons — thematic / decorative -->
<span class="i-game-icons-open-book text-4xl text-muted"></span>

<!-- Simple Icons — brand -->
<span class="i-simple-icons-mastodon text-lg hover:text-violet"></span>

Keep the icon set small. Every icon not in this list must be added to the safelist in uno.config.js to survive the UnoCSS tree-shake.

docs.title: UI Icons — Lucide
docs.text: Used for all interface controls. Never use Lucide icons decoratively — they are functional and should always carry a readable label or aria-label.

docs.title: Thematic Icons — Game Icons
docs.text:
Used for empty states, section illustrations, and decorative accents. Never use for functional controls — these icons are for atmosphere, not interaction.

Game Icons are free under CC BY 3.0. Loaded via @iconify-json/game-icons.

Concept Icon Usage Games / Campaigns i-game-icons-open-book Empty state — no games Session Reports i-game-icons-scroll-quill Empty state — no reports Characters / NPCs i-game-icons-two-shadows Empty state — no characters Quotes i-game-icons-chat-bubble Empty state — no quotes Dice / Game system i-game-icons-dice-six-faces-four Game system badge Claim link i-game-icons-manacles Claim relationship art Adopt link i-game-icons-hand Adopt relationship art Fork link i-game-icons-divergence Fork relationship art Federation i-game-icons-rss Federation / ActivityPub Light mode — thematic icons at empty-state scale open-book scroll-quill two-shadows chat-bubble dice-six-faces-four manaclesClaim handAdopt divergenceFork Dark mode open-book scroll-quill two-shadows chat-bubble dice-six-faces-four manaclesClaim handAdopt divergenceFork

docs.title: Import & Export
docs.text:
Suddenly lets you export your games and characters as JSON files for backup or instance migration. These files can be re-imported on any Suddenly instance.

Reports are not included

Reports (comptes-rendus) are federated via ActivityPub and available through subscriptions — they are not part of the personal data export.

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
Go to Settings → Data and click Download my games (JSON).

Only local games you own are included (remote=false).

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
[
  {
    "title": "City of Mist — Season 2",
    "created_at": "2024-03-15T10:00:00+00:00",
    "description": "Urban fantasy noir campaign set in a city of living myths.",
    "game_system": "City of Mist",
    "is_public": true
  },
  {
    "title": "Ironsworn: Starforged",
    "created_at": "2024-07-01T08:30:00+00:00",
    "description": "",
    "game_system": "Starforged",
    "is_public": false
  }
]

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Notes title string ✅ Max 200 characters created_at ISO 8601 datetime ✅ Used for deduplication — do not alter description string — Defaults to "" if omitted game_system string — Max 100 characters. Defaults to "" if omitted is_public boolean — Defaults to true if omitted

docs.title: Import rules
docs.text:
• Items without title are skipped
  • Items without created_at are created without deduplication check
  • If a game with the same (title, created_at) already exists → skipped (idempotent)
  • Otherwise → created with a new UUID; the original created_at is preserved
  • Unknown fields are ignored silently

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
Go to Settings → Data and click Download my characters (JSON).

All characters where you are the creator are exported, regardless of their current status (NPC, PC, claimed, adopted, forked).

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
[
  {
    "name": "Vasquez",
    "origin_game_title": "City of Mist — Season 2",
    "created_at": "2024-03-20T14:30:00+00:00",
    "description": "Former detective, now private investigator in the Mist.",
    "status": "npc",
    "sheet_url": null
  },
  {
    "name": "The Archivist",
    "origin_game_title": "City of Mist — Season 2",
    "created_at": "2024-04-10T09:15:00+00:00",
    "description": "Keeper of forbidden maps. Speaks only in riddles.",
    "status": "adopted",
    "sheet_url": "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/..."
  }
]

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Notes name string ✅ Max 100 characters origin_game_title string ✅ Must exactly match the title of a game owned by the importing user created_at ISO 8601 datetime ✅ Used for deduplication — do not alter description string — Defaults to "" if omitted status string — One of: npc pc claimed adopted forked. Defaults to npc sheet_url string (URL) — Defaults to null if omitted

docs.title: Import rules
docs.text:
• Items without name are skipped
  • origin_game_title must exactly match (case-sensitive) a game owned by the importing user → skipped if not found
  • If a character with the same (name, created_at) already exists → skipped (idempotent)
  • Otherwise → created with a new UUID and auto-generated slug; the original created_at is preserved
  • owner is set to the importing user only when status is pc
  • Unknown fields are ignored silently

docs.title: Migration workflow
docs.text:
When moving your account to another instance:

Order matters

Always import games first, then characters. Characters reference a game by title — if the game doesn't exist yet on the new instance, the character will be skipped.

1. Settings → Data → Download my games (JSON)
2. Settings → Data → Download my characters (JSON)
3. On the new instance: import games
4. On the new instance: import characters

Follows use the Mastodon-compatible CSV format (separate flow, also in Settings → Data).

docs.title: Creating files programmatically
docs.text:
Both formats are plain JSON arrays. You can generate them from any tool or script as long as you respect the required fields and the ISO 8601 datetime format with timezone offset.

Minimal valid games file:

[{ "title": "My Campaign", "created_at": "2025-01-01T00:00:00+00:00" }]

Minimal valid characters file:

[{
  "name": "My Character",
  "origin_game_title": "My Campaign",
  "created_at": "2025-01-02T00:00:00+00:00"
}]

Timezone

Use UTC (+00:00) whenever possible. Datetimes without a timezone offset may cause deduplication to behave unexpectedly.

docs.title: Contributing Translations
docs.text: Suddenly's interface is built on Django's standard i18n system. English is the source language (msgid); French ships as the primary translation. Adding a new language requires no code changes — only a .po file.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Install gettext (provides msgfmt, msginit, msgmerge):

# Debian / Ubuntu
sudo apt install gettext

# macOS
brew install gettext

# Windows (Chocolatey)
choco install gettext

Verify: which msgfmt (or msgfmt --version on Windows).

docs.title: Adding a new language (example: Spanish)
docs.text:
# 1. Create the .po file for your language
python manage.py makemessages -l es --no-wrap \
    --ignore=venv --ignore=node_modules --ignore=staticfiles

# 2. Translate strings
#    Use locale/fr/LC_MESSAGES/django.po as a reference for context.
#    Open locale/es/LC_MESSAGES/django.po in your editor or Poedit.

# 3. Compile
python manage.py compilemessages -l es

# 4. Test locally
LANGUAGE_CODE=es python manage.py runserver
# Switch language via the nav switcher or set Accept-Language: es in your browser.

docs.title: Declaring the language in settings
docs.text:
Add your language code to config/settings/base.py:

LANGUAGES = [
    ("en", "English"),
    ("fr", "Français"),
    ("es", "Español"),   # ← add your language here
]

This makes it available in the nav switcher and the profile language selector.

docs.title: Translation guidelines
docs.text: Rule Detail UI strings only Never translate user-generated content (game titles, bios, character names) Keep msgid stable Never edit English source strings in a PR that only translates — open a separate PR for source changes Plural forms Declare Plural-Forms correctly for your language in the .po header No fuzzy entries Review all #, fuzzy entries and either confirm or remove the flag before submitting No empty msgstr Every msgid must have a non-empty msgstr (or be intentionally left empty for en.po only)

docs.title: Recommended tool: Poedit
docs.text: Poedit provides a GUI for .po files with spell-check and plural form helpers.

docs.title: PR checklist
docs.text:
[ ] locale/<lang>/LC_MESSAGES/django.po updated and complete
[ ] No #, fuzzy entries remaining
[ ] No empty msgstr (except en.po which uses msgid as-is)
[ ] Plural-Forms header correct for the target language
[ ] LANGUAGES updated in config/settings/base.py
[ ] Tested locally: interface renders in the new language
[ ] python manage.py compilemessages -l <lang> runs without errors

docs.title: Keeping catalogs up to date
docs.text:
When new UI strings are added to the codebase, all .po files go out of date. Run:

python manage.py makemessages -l <lang> --no-wrap \
    --ignore=venv --ignore=node_modules --ignore=staticfiles

Then translate the new msgid entries (they appear without a msgstr). The CI guardrail (make i18n-check) will catch any .po files that are out of sync.
